compatibilityLabel: React DOM 19.2.6 delegated event registration and dispatch oracle

generation.method: checked runtime inventory plus exact npm tarballs extracted into a temporary node_modules tree and probed through a deterministic minimal DOM host
generation.probeIsolation: one Node child process per React DOM event scenario and mode
generation.pathNormalization: temporary extraction paths and React randomized listener marker names are not serialized

probeModes.reason: Default Node development probes delegated React DOM event registration and synthetic event dispatch with development diagnostics enabled.
Default Node production probes the same delegated event behavior against the production React DOM client bundle.

eventExamples.reason: Click is the canonical delegated discrete mouse event used for capture, bubble, stopPropagation, preventDefault, and synthetic event shape evidence.
Mouse move is a delegated continuous mouse event with normal capture and bubble dispatch.
Wheel is a delegated continuous event whose root listener registration uses passive browser event options when support is detected.

scenarios.expectedBehavior: Parent capture, child capture, child bubble, then parent bubble are invoked from one delegated click dispatch.
Calling stopPropagation in the child bubble listener prevents the parent bubble listener while preserving earlier capture listeners.
Calling preventDefault in a synthetic click listener updates both synthetic and native default-prevented state.
A delegated click creates a persistent SyntheticMouseEvent-compatible object with stable target and callback-scoped currentTarget.
A delegated continuous mousemove dispatch uses the same capture and bubble listener ordering as click.
A delegated wheel dispatch exposes wheel delta fields and pairs with passive root listener installation evidence.

intentionalGaps.reason: This oracle records pinned React DOM 19.2.6 behavior only; Fast React DOM event implementation is not in this worker scope.
Event priority lane claims are deliberately excluded because worker 048 owns the event-priority oracle files.

The probe host implements deterministic DOM APIs needed for createRoot and delegated event dispatch; browser-specific focus, selection, IME, form, and layout behavior require later browser-backed or jsdom-backed oracles.
Hydration replay, portal retargeting, non-delegated scroll/media events, and plugin-specific form/change/select/beforeinput behavior are separate React DOM surfaces.
